elements.sent-2411-001: Building on our recent work we construct the Penrose transformations of the cohomology groups of homogenous line bundles on flag domains $D=G_\R/T$ with $G_\R$ of Hermitian type.
elements.sent-2411-002: We give sufficient conditions for the injectivity of the Penrose transformations, and conditions under which the Penrose transformation of the automorphic cohomology groups on compact quotients of flag domains is an isomorphism.
elements.sent-2411-003: Finally we prove that the higher automorphic cohomology groups of certain homogeneous line bundles are isomorphic to the groups of automorphic forms on the Hermitian symmetric domain.
elements.sent-2411-004: As noted in the preface of \cite{BE , the Penrose transformation was originally introduced by Roger Penrose in the 1960s as an isomorphism between a sheaf cohomology group on a region of projective space and the solutions of a zero-rest-mass field equation on a region of spacetime in mathematical physics.}
elements.sent-2411-005: Since its introduction, numerous generalizations of the Penrose transformation have been studied in both mathematics and physics.
elements.sent-2411-006: In recent works \cite{C1 , \cite{C2 , and \cite{C3 by Carayol, as well as in \cite{GGK and \cite{GGK14 by Green, Griffiths, and Kerr, the Penrose transformation has been studied on flag domains for $SU(2,1)$ and $Sp(4)$.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-007: In the context of Hodge theory and representation theory, the Penrose transformation on flag domains investigates the relationship between the cohomology groups of homogeneous line bundles on a classical flag domain $D'$ and those on the non-classical flag domain $D$ that is diffeomorphic to $D'$.
elements.sent-2411-008: Additionally, it also studies the relations between the corresponding automorphic cohomology groups on the compact quotients $X$ and $X'$ of $D$ and $D'$ respectively.
elements.sent-2411-009: In our recent work \cite{LiuShen24 , we proved that any non-classical flag domain $D = G_\R / V$ with $G_\R$ of Hermitian type is diffeomorphic to a classical flag domain $D'$, where $G_\R$ is a semisimple real Lie group and $V \subset G_\R$ is a compact subgroup containing a compact Cartan subgroup $T$.}
elements.sent-2411-010: This represents the most general result on this topic, since any real Lie group $G_\R$ acting on a classical flag domain must be of Hermitian type.
elements.sent-2411-011: Furthermore, in \cite{LiuShen24 , we also showed that the compact, smooth quotient $X$ of $D$ admits no $\partial \bar{\partial}$-structure compatible with the complex structure induced from $D$.}
elements.sent-2411-012: This result is achieved by proving a conjecture of Green--Griffiths--Kerr concerning the vanishing of the automorphic cohomology groups of any non-trivial homogeneous vector bundles on $X$.
elements.sent-2411-013: In contrast, it was proved in \cite{GS that the compact quotient $X'$ of the classical flag domain $D'$ is a projective manifold, and thus, it has an arithmetic structure on its automorphic cohomology group.}
elements.sent-2411-014: Once the Penrose transformation is shown to be an isomorphism, the automorphic cohomology group on $X$ will inherit the arithmetic structure from that on $X'$.
elements.sent-2411-015: Therefore, in conjunction with our work \cite{LiuShen24 , the Penrose transformation on flag domains reveals unexpected properties.}
elements.sent-2411-016: For further applications of the Penrose transformation, we refer the reader to \cite{GGK , Lecture 9, and \cite{GGK14 .}}

elements.sent-2411-068: The paper is organized as follows.
elements.sent-2411-069: In Section \ref{Pre we introduce the basic notations of the geometry of flag domains and review our results on new complex structures of non-classical flag domains.}
elements.sent-2411-070: In Section \ref{Corres we introduce the corresponding space and the incidence variety for the flag domains and relate the cohomology groups of the homogenous line bundles on flag domains to the de Rham cohomology groups on the corresponding space.}
elements.sent-2411-071: An important vanishing theorem is proved in this section, which is crucial to the proof of the injectivity of the Penrose transformation.
elements.sent-2411-072: In Section \ref{section Pen we define the Penrose transformation on flag domains and establish its injectivity under certain condition.}
elements.sent-2411-073: In Section \ref{section Pen compact we introduce the Penrose transformation on the compact quotients of flag domains and prove that it is an isomorphism under the conditions given in Section \ref{section Pen and the conditions induced by Property W of Williams.}}
elements.sent-2411-074: We also apply our result to the groups of automorphic forms on Hermitian symmetric domains which can be identified with the higher automorphic cohomology groups of certain line bundles on the compact quotient of the non-classical flag domain.

elements.sent-2411-387: Building on our recent work, we construct the Penrose transformations of the cohomology groups of homogeneous line bundles on flag domains $D = G_\R / T$, where $G_\R$ is of Hermitian type.
elements.sent-2411-388: We provide sufficient conditions for the injectivity of the Penrose transformation and identify conditions under which the Penrose transformation of the automorphic cohomology groups on compact quotients of flag domains is an isomorphism.
elements.sent-2411-389: Finally, we prove that the higher automorphic cohomology groups of certain homogeneous line bundles are isomorphic to the groups of automorphic forms on the Hermitian symmetric domain, and we apply this result to the cup products of the automorphic cohomology groups.

elements.sent-2411-391: As mentioned on Page 223 and Page 233 of \cite{GGK , little is known about the Penrose transformation on flag domains, except for some special cases.}
elements.sent-2411-392: This result is obtained by proving a conjecture of Green--Griffiths--Kerr concerning the vanishing of the zeroth automorphic cohomology groups of any non-trivial homogeneous vector bundles on $X$.
elements.sent-2411-393: Therefore, in conjunction with our work \cite{LiuShen24 , the Penrose transformation on flag domains reveals unexpected useful properties.}
